data.title: As a developer, pre-commit is a required dependency and hooks are installed as the final step after all artifacts are committed
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add pre-commit to Step 0 required dep check (alongside bash, coreutils, git). Move hook installation to the final group position (group 5 of 6 per S4's batch structure, after all other artifacts are committed). The hook installation commit bypasses review and test gate checks since hooks are pre-built plugin components, not custom code changes.

**Why**: SC5. Fresh-repo onboarding currently deadlocks because hooks run on the initial commit before the test index exists. Moving hook install last, and adding pre-commit as required dep, fixes the deadlock and ensures hooks are always present after a successful onboarding.

**Scope**:
- IN: pre-commit added to Step 0 required dep check, hook install resequenced to group 5 (after S4 stabilizes the batch script structure), hook commit bypasses review/test gate checks
- OUT: hook script content (unchanged), Husky/pre-commit-framework/bare hooks detection logic (unchanged)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, pre-commit is listed and checked in Step 0 dep scan alongside bash, coreutils, and git; onboarding halts with a clear install instruction if pre-commit is missing
  <- Satisfies: "Pre-commit is treated as a required dependency checked at start alongside bash and coreutils"
- When this story is complete, hooks are installed in group 5 (the hook install script), after all other onboarding artifacts have been committed in groups 1-4
  <- Satisfies: "hooks are installed as the final step after all other onboarding artifacts are committed"
- When this story is complete, a fresh-repo onboarding run (on an empty repo with no prior commits) completes without any hook-induced commit failure
  <- Satisfies: "a fresh-repo run completes without any hook-induced commit failure"
- When this story is complete, the hook installation commit bypasses the review and test gate checks (hooks are pre-built plugin components, not custom code changes)
  <- Satisfies: "the hook installation commit skips review and test gate verification since the committed hooks are pre-built plugin components"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Interrupted onboarding between group 4 and group 5 leaves artifacts committed with no hooks yet installed — the skill should detect this state on re-run and offer to resume from group 5
- [Reliability] Hook orphan on fresh repo — validate git working tree state before the hook install commit to prevent installing hooks on the wrong branch

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
